mission.title: cert-manager: Wildcard certificates support
mission.description: Wildcard certificates support. Requested by 26+ users.

steps.title: Check current cert-manager deployment
steps.description:
Verify your cert-manager version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
helm list -n cert-manager 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working cert-manager installation.

steps.title: Review cert-manager configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cert-manager configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
kubectl get configmap -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cert-manager
```
Are there any plans to support wildcard certificates?

https://letsencrypt.org/2017/07/06/wildcard-certificates-coming-jan-2018.html

steps.title: Apply the fix for Wildcard certificates support
steps.description:
:

Adds support for the ACME v2 protocol in favour of v1

:

~This PR is not ready

. We need to validate and ensure boulder runs with the v2 protocol enabled during our e2e tests. In future we can switch to something like [pebble](https://github.com/letsencrypt/pebble), a lightweight alternative
```yaml
Certificate:
    Data:
        Version: 3 (0x2)
        Serial Number:
            fa:f5:cc:92:f2:02:15:58:37:05:02:9f:bf:53:9f:9c:d4:0c
    Signature Algorithm: sha256WithRSAEncryption
        Issuer: CN=Fake LE Intermediate X1
        Validity
            Not Before: Mar 23 15:56:39 2018 GMT
            Not After : Jun 21 15:56:39 2018 GMT
        Subject: CN=*.james-gcp.redacted.net
        Subject Public Key Info:
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Wildcard certificates support" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
:

Adds support for the ACME v2 protocol in favour of v1

:

~This PR is not ready

. We need to validate and ensure boulder runs with the v2 protocol enabled during our e2e tests.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cert-manager installed or the issue environment reproducible.
